skill.name: lead-research-assistant
skill.description: Researches qualified sales leads, scores account fit, and suggests outreach strategies for business development teams.
skill.summary: Finds and prioritizes sales leads with fit scores and outreach guidance.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, template placeholders, and ordinary lead research text. No executable script, prompt injection attempt, credential access, or malicious exfiltration pattern was found in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the skill asks the assistant to inspect local product context and research public company information.

low_findings.title: Local Product Context May Include Sensitive Details
low_findings.description: The skill suggests running from a product source directory and analyzing the repository to understand the product. This is useful for lead targeting, but users should avoid including secrets, private customer data, or unreleased strategy in generated lead reports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly ask the assistant to analyze the user's codebase for product context. The risk is limited because there is no instruction to transmit secrets or execute code.

low_findings.title: Public Lead Research Requires Privacy Review
low_findings.description: The skill asks for company research, decision-maker roles, LinkedIn URLs, and outreach strategies. This can be legitimate sales research, but users should verify compliance with privacy rules and outreach policies.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill's intended workflow includes public research and contact strategy generation. This is not malicious, but it creates normal compliance risk for lead generation.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command-execution locations are fenced Markdown examples, and the weak-cryptography alerts map to ordinary words and placeholders. No evidence of Ruby backtick execution, shell commands, or cryptographic code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged areas are visible as Markdown examples and prose after reviewing the file with line numbers. There is no executable file or command syntax outside documentation fences.

content.value_statement: Finding credible prospects takes time and often produces shallow account lists. This skill structures lead research, ranks companies by fit, and suggests outreach angles.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes a product or service description to define an ideal customer profile.
Researches companies that match industry, location, size, and technology criteria.
Scores leads from 1 to 10 with fit explanations and timing signals.
Suggests target roles, value propositions, and conversation starters for outreach.
Formats lead research into a clear, scannable account list.
Can use repository context to infer product features when the user permits it.
limitations: It depends on available public information and may miss private company context.
It does not verify email addresses, phone numbers, or CRM records by itself.
It should not be used to collect sensitive personal data without a lawful basis.
Lead scores are research judgments and should be reviewed by a sales owner.

use_cases.title: Build a target account list
use_cases.description: Create a prioritized list of companies that match a product, market, and location brief.

use_cases.title: Research partnership prospects
use_cases.description: Find companies with complementary products and explain why a partnership could be valuable.

use_cases.title: Validate an ideal customer profile
use_cases.description: Compare real companies against a draft customer profile and identify stronger qualification signals.

prompt_templates.title: Find basic leads
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a product description and market.
prompt_templates.prompt: I sell [product or service]. Find 10 companies in [industry or location] that may be strong leads. Include why each company fits.

prompt_templates.title: Use a customer profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this ideal customer profile: [industry], [company size], [location], [pain points], and [tools used]. Find 15 qualified leads and rank them by fit.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare outreach angles
prompt_templates.scenario: Lead list with contact strategy support.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research companies that match [criteria]. For each lead, include target role, likely pain point, value proposition, and two conversation starters.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze product context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow using repository or product context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the product context in this repository, avoid secrets, and identify 20 target accounts that would benefit from this product. Explain the scoring criteria.

output_examples.input: Find leads for a privacy tool that redacts sensitive data before prompts are sent to AI coding assistants.
output_examples.output: A ranked lead list for fintech, healthcare, and legal software companies, with fit reasons, target roles, and outreach angles.

output_examples.input: Find Bay Area companies that may need consulting for remote team productivity.
output_examples.output: A summary of companies with remote hiring signals, priority scores, likely decision makers, and tailored conversation starters.

output_examples.input: Use my ideal customer profile to identify ecommerce brands that need analytics automation.
output_examples.output: A qualified account list grouped by priority, with public signals, business context, and next-step research recommendations.

best_practices: Provide a clear product description, target market, and exclusion criteria.
Ask for evidence behind each lead score so results can be reviewed.
Review privacy and outreach rules before using contact details in campaigns.
anti_patterns: Do not request scraped personal contact data without consent or a lawful basis.
Do not treat fit scores as final sales qualification without human review.
Do not include secrets, customer data, or unreleased plans in the research prompt.

faq.question: Can this skill find decision makers?
faq.answer: It can suggest relevant roles and public profiles when available. Users should verify all contact details before outreach.

faq.question: Does it work for local businesses?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the service, geography, business type, and any constraints for a local prospect list.

faq.question: Can it use my repository for context?
faq.answer: Yes, if the assistant has access and you permit review. Remove secrets and private customer data first.

faq.question: Does it send emails or automate outreach?
faq.answer: No. It prepares research and strategy. Sending outreach requires a separate tool or manual workflow.

faq.question: How accurate are the lead scores?
faq.answer: Scores are based on visible signals and stated criteria. A sales owner should validate priority before action.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
